# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Tendering (PR D) — unified communications panel

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-26.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

The Tender Detail → Overview tab currently has three separate panels:

- Activity timeline ("Add a note…" + Post)
- Clarifications & Communications (+ Add entry — RFI / note / etc.)
- Follow-ups (detail + due date + Add)

The owner wants these merged into ONE unified panel with:

- A single chronological feed showing all entries (default view).
- A tabbed view as an alternative — filter chips at the top let users pivot.
- Per-entry **type** selected via dropdown at creation: `Note` / `RFI` /
  `Email` / `Call` / `Meeting` / `Follow-up` / `Self-reminder` / `Task`.
- Optional fields per type (due date, assignee, status) appear conditionally.
- Task assignment to other users — currently all users are office workers
  ("dashboard users"); a future role split will exclude site workers.
- **Notifications** when a task is assigned to someone other than the
  author: BOTH in-app AND email.

This is the largest of the brainstormed items — new schema, data migration,
UI redesign, notification flow.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `feat/tendering-unified-comms-panel`.
- Conflict-check touched files extensively — this PR touches the tender
  detail page, the API, the data model, and notification flows.
- **Migration naming**: use full `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*` timestamps for ALL
  migrations in this PR. Run `pnpm --filter @project-ops/api prisma migrate
  dev --name <slug>` to auto-generate the timestamp (do not hand-name folders
  with bare `YYYYMMDD_*` — those sort before timestamped folders for the same
  day and break ordering). The schema migration must be generated BEFORE any
  data backfill so timestamps sort correctly.

## Schema — new `TenderEntry` table

Add to `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`:

```prisma
model TenderEntry {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenderId    String   @map("tender_id")
  tender      Tender   @relation(fields: [tenderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  type        String   // 'note' | 'rfi' | 'email' | 'call' | 'meeting' | 'follow_up' | 'self_reminder' | 'task'
  subject     String?
  body        String
  dueDate     DateTime? @map("due_date")          // populated for follow_up / self_reminder / task
  assigneeId  String?   @map("assignee_id")       // populated for task; null otherwise
  assignee    User?     @relation("TenderEntryAssignee", fields: [assigneeId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  status      String    @default("open")          // 'open' | 'done' | 'cancelled'
  authorId    String   @map("author_id")
  author      User     @relation("TenderEntryAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
  @@index([tenderId, createdAt])
  @@index([assigneeId, status])
  @@map("tender_entries")
}
```

Add the inverse relations on `Tender` and `User`. Migration: auto-generated
full timestamp (`pnpm prisma migrate dev --name tender_entries`). The data
backfill below should be a SEPARATE migration generated AFTER this one so its
timestamp sorts later (Prisma applies migrations alphabetically by folder
name — bare `YYYYMMDD_*` folders sort before `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*` and will run
before the table they depend on).

## Data migration

Backfill `tender_entries` from existing data, idempotent (skip if already
migrated). Run inside the same migration or a paired data migration:

- Each row in `tender_notes` → `TenderEntry` with `type='note'`,
  `body=tender_notes.body`, `authorId=tender_notes.author_user_id`,
  timestamps preserved.
- Each row in `tender_clarifications` / `tender_clarification_notes` →
  `TenderEntry` with `type` derived: `'rfi'` for RFI-shaped rows;
  `'email'` / `'call'` / `'meeting'` / `'note'` for the matching
  `noteType`. Subject + body mapped from the existing fields.
- Each row in `tender_follow_ups` → `TenderEntry` with `type='follow_up'`,
  `body=details`, `dueDate=dueAt`, `assigneeId=assignedUserId` (if set),
  `status` mapped from existing status.

**Legacy tables stay in place** for one release cycle. A follow-up PR drops
them after one release of bake time. Do NOT drop them in this PR.

## API

Under `/api/v1/tenders/:id/entries`:

- `GET` — list with query filters: `type`, `assigneeId`, `status`, `from`,
  `to`. Default sort: `createdAt DESC`.
- `POST` — create. Body:
  ```json
  { "type": "task", "subject": "...", "body": "...",
    "dueDate": "2026-06-01", "assigneeId": "user-..." }
  ```
  Validate that conditional fields are present for the relevant types
  (e.g. `assigneeId` required when `type='task'`).
- `PATCH /:entryId` — update.
- `DELETE /:entryId` — hard delete OR set `status='cancelled'` depending on
  what the existing tender-data delete conventions do.

Use the existing tender-access permission guard. Swagger decorators per
CLAUDE.md. DTOs with `class-validator`.

## UI

Replace the three existing panels on the Overview tab with one component:
`apps/web/src/pages/tendering/TenderEntriesPanel.tsx`.

### Layout

- Header: *"Activity & communications"* + `+ Add entry` button.
- Filter chips: `All` · `Notes` · `Correspondence (RFI / Email / Call / Meeting)` · `Follow-ups` · `My Tasks`.
- View toggle: **Feed** (chronological, newest first — default) ↔ **Tabs** (one
  tab per type group). The user's preferred view persists per session via
  localStorage.
- Each entry row shows:
  - Type badge (coloured pill).
  - Subject (if any) + body preview (truncate at 2 lines, expand on click).
  - Author + timestamp.
  - Type-specific affordances:
    - Tasks / follow-ups / reminders: due date pill + assignee chip (where
      applicable) + status pill (open / done / cancelled — click to toggle).
    - Email / Call / Meeting: optional metadata (no specific affordance
      required beyond the body).

### Add-entry form

- **Type** dropdown (required) — selecting the type reveals the
  conditional optional fields below.
- **Subject** (optional, for all types).
- **Body** (required).
- **Due date** (rendered only for `follow_up` / `self_reminder` / `task`).
- **Assignee** picker (rendered only for `task`) — loads from
  `GET /users` (filter by role if a role classification exists; today all
  users are "dashboard users" / office workers, so an unfiltered list is
  fine. A future PR will add the office-vs-site role split).

Submit: `POST /tenders/:id/entries`. Optimistic UI; revert on failure.

## Notifications

When a `TenderEntry` is created with `type='task'` AND
`assigneeId !== authorId`:

- **In-app notification** for the assignee — create a row in the existing
  notifications table (look for `Notification` model / `NotificationService`
  in the API; the top-bar bell already shows unread count).
- **Email** to the assignee — task subject, body, due date, link back to the
  tender. Use the existing `EmailService` / `MailService` if present. Subject
  line example: *"[Project Ops] New task on tender IS-T020 — {subject}"*.

If either the in-app notifications or the email infrastructure isn't yet
present in usable form: **stop and report back in the PR body** — don't
scaffold a new notification system blind. Owner will decide whether to
build it as part of this PR or factor it out.

## Tests

- Schema migration: legacy rows correctly seeded into `tender_entries`.
- Endpoints: CRUD + filter combinations + conditional-field validation +
  permission enforcement.
- UI unit: type-conditional fields render (due date for follow-ups etc.).
- Integration: assigning a task fires both the in-app notification and the
  email (mocked sender).
- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.

## PR

- Title: `[5A] Tendering — unified communications panel`
- Body: schema change, migration semantics, UI redesign, notification flow,
  legacy-table deprecation plan (drop in follow-up PR after one release of
  bake time).
- Reviewer `GH-Mantova`.
- **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies on a real tender: create one entry of
  each type, filter by type, assign a task, confirm both in-app + email
  notifications fire, confirm the legacy panels are gone.

## Scope

Only the unified panel + new `tender_entries` table + data migration +
notification wiring on task assignment. Do not drop the legacy
`tender_notes` / `tender_clarifications` / `tender_follow_ups` tables —
they stay one release cycle for safety.
